instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. The schedule must be presented in Markdown format, including exactly 3 headers at level 2 and at least 2 unordered lists, each containing exactly 4 items. Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule. Use the keyword "update" as a verb. Ensure the tone of the schedule is formal and professional throughout. The response must contain exactly 8 sentences, with the first paragraph containing exactly 3 sentences and the second paragraph containing exactly 5 sentences. Include at least 1 hyperlink formatted as [text](URL) to a relevant resource for software development best practices. Avoid using the word "brainstorming" in the schedule.
response:
The team will commence the week with a collaborative planning session to define the scope of the new app feature. A dedicated collaborative brainstorming session will be scheduled on Monday morning to align on technical requirements. This initial phase ensures that all stakeholders are prepared for the upcoming development cycle.

Coding activities will dominate Tuesday through Thursday, with daily stand-ups to track progress. The team must update the project management board after each completed task to maintain transparency. Testing protocols will be implemented on Friday to identify any critical bugs before deployment. Please refer to [Software Development Life Cycle Best Practices](https://www.agilealliance.org/glossary/sdlc) for further guidance on our methodology. Finally, the week concludes with a review meeting to discuss lessons learned and plan the next iteration.

Conflicts between Keyword Constraints an.new_constraint: Avoid using the word "brainstorming" in the schedule.
Conflicts between Keyword Constraints an.org_constraint: Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule.
